color_coding.RED: Very Important — Must Memorize for NEET

top_traps.trap: Halogens (Cl, Br, F, I) are ORTHO/PARA directors — NOT meta. Students write meta = WRONG.
Anti-Markovnikov (peroxide effect) works ONLY for HBr. Not HCl, not HI.
Lindlar catalyst = CIS alkene. Na/liquid NH3 = TRANS alkene. Students swap these.
Methane CANNOT be prepared by Kolbe electrolysis.
trans isomer has HIGHER melting point; cis isomer has HIGHER boiling point.
C-C bond in benzene = 139 pm (not 133 pm, not 154 pm).
Internal alkynes (but-2-yne) do NOT react with NaNH2. Only terminal alkynes.
Hydration of ethyne gives ETHANAL (not ethanol) — via vinyl alcohol tautomerism.
BHC (Gammaxane) = ADDITION reaction of benzene + Cl2 under UV. NOT substitution.

alkanes.name: Alkanes
alkanes.other_name: Paraffins (parum affinis = little affinity in Latin)

quick_facts.first_member: CH4 (Methane) — found in coal mines and marshy places
quick_facts.solubility: Non-polar; insoluble in water; soluble in non-polar solvents
quick_facts.reactivity: Inert to acids, bases, oxidising/reducing agents under normal conditions

isomerism.description: Same molecular formula, different carbon chain structure

boiling_point_rule.reason: More branching → more spherical shape → less surface area contact → weaker van der Waals forces

preparation.conditions: Pt/Pd at room temperature; Ni requires higher temperature and pressure

preparation.note: Works for Cl, Br, I — NOT for fluorides

preparation.important: ONLY useful for EVEN carbon alkanes using the SAME alkyl halide
preparation.trap: Two DIFFERENT alkyl halides give mixture of R-R, R-R' and R'-R' — not selective

preparation.important: Product has ONE CARBON LESS than the parent carboxylic acid
preparation.trap: Sodium propanoate (3C) gives ETHANE (2C) — not propane

preparation.mechanism: At anode: RCOO(-) --> RCOO(.) --> R(.) + CO2 --> R-R
preparation.important: Gives EVEN carbon alkanes at the anode
preparation.trap: Methane CANNOT be made by Kolbe method — formate gives only CO2, no CH3 radical

halogenation.name: Free Radical Halogenation

special_notes.I2: Too slow, reversible — needs HIO3 or HNO3 as oxidising agent

steps.name: INITIATION
steps.note: Cl-Cl bond weakest; breaks first by light/heat

steps.name: TERMINATION
steps.note: ETHANE byproduct — most important NEET point

halogenation.neet_trap: Ethane is a BYPRODUCT of methane chlorination due to termination step 3b

combustion.general_equation: CnH2n+2 + [(3n+1)/2] O2 --> n CO2 + (n+1) H2O + large heat
combustion.incomplete_combustion: Produces carbon black — used in ink, printer ink, pigments, filters

aromatization.name: Aromatization / Reforming
aromatization.reaction: n-Hexane --[Cr2O3 or V2O5, Al2O3, 773K, 10-20 atm]--> Benzene + 3H2

pyrolysis.name: Pyrolysis / Cracking
pyrolysis.reaction: Higher alkane --[high temp]--> lower alkanes + alkenes

conformations.description: Spatial arrangements of atoms by rotation around C-C single bond
conformations.important_fact: Bond angles and bond lengths are SAME in all conformations. Only spatial arrangement changes.
conformations.trap: Students write eclipsed as more stable — WRONG. Staggered is always more stable.

projections.sawhorse: C-C drawn as long line. Front carbon at bottom, rear at top.
projections.newman: View head-on along C-C axis. Front carbon = dot; rear carbon = circle.

alkenes.name: Alkenes
alkenes.other_name: Olefins (oil forming — C2H4 + Cl2 gives oily liquid)

quick_facts.nature: Source of loosely held pi electrons — attacked by electrophiles

structure_of_double_bond.sigma_bond: head-on overlap of sp2 orbitals (strong, ~397 kJ/mol)
structure_of_double_bond.geometry: All 6 atoms in ethene (2C + 4H) lie in the SAME PLANE (planar molecule)

geometrical_isomerism.condition: BOTH doubly bonded carbons must have TWO DIFFERENT substituents
geometrical_isomerism.no_isomerism_when: One doubly bonded C has IDENTICAL groups (e.g. CH2= or =CR2)

cis_isomer.group_arrangement: Same groups on SAME side of C=C

trans_isomer.group_arrangement: Same groups on OPPOSITE sides of C=C

reactions.reagent: Lindlar catalyst (Pd/C + quinoline or sulphur compound)
reactions.note: Partially deactivated catalyst — stops at alkene stage

preparation.trap: Most repeated NEET trap: Students swap Lindlar and Na/NH3 products

preparation.note: Beta-elimination: H removed from beta carbon
Vicinal = halogen on adjacent (1,2) carbon atoms
Beta-elimination; OH takes the beta-H and leaves as water

addition_of_halogens.test_for_unsaturation: REDDISH ORANGE colour of Br2/CCl4 DECOLOURISES = TEST FOR UNSATURATION
addition_of_halogens.note: Iodine does NOT add to alkenes under normal conditions

markovnikov_addition.name: Markovnikov's Rule
markovnikov_addition.rule: Negative part (X-) adds to carbon with FEWER H atoms (more substituted). H+ adds to carbon with MORE H atoms.
markovnikov_addition.mechanism: H+ attacks C=C --> more stable 2° carbocation forms (not 1°) --> Br- attacks --> 2-bromopropane

anti_markovnikov_addition.name: Anti-Markovnikov / Peroxide Effect / Kharash Effect
anti_markovnikov_addition.applies_to: HBr ONLY with peroxide — NOT HCl, NOT HI

mechanism_steps.reaction: ROOR --> 2 RO(.) | RO(.) + HBr --> ROH + Br(.)

mechanism_steps.note: Br adds; 2° radical more stable than 1°

anti_markovnikov_addition.why_not_HCl: H-Cl bond (430 kJ/mol) too strong for radical cleavage
anti_markovnikov_addition.why_not_HI: I radicals recombine to form I2 instead of adding to double bond
anti_markovnikov_addition.discovered_by: Kharash and Mayo, 1933, University of Chicago

addition_of_sulphuric_acid.reaction: CH2=CH2 + H2SO4 (cold conc.) --> CH3-CH2-OSO3H (Ethyl hydrogen sulphate)

baeyer_test.test: Pink KMnO4 decolourises = positive test for unsaturation

acidic_KMnO4.product: Ketones and/or carboxylic acids depending on substitution

ozonolysis.name: Ozonolysis
ozonolysis.rule: Ozonolysis cleaves C=C bond. Each carbon of C=C becomes a CARBONYL carbon.

quick_facts.bond_composition: 1 sigma + 2 pi bonds between the two carbons

structure_of_triple_bond.sigma_bonds: C-C sigma (sp-sp overlap) + C-H sigma (sp-1s overlap)
structure_of_triple_bond.pi_bonds: 2 pi bonds from lateral overlap of 2px-2px and 2py-2py orbitals
structure_of_triple_bond.electron_cloud: Cylindrically symmetrical — ethyne is a linear molecule

preparation.method: From Calcium Carbide (Industrial Method for Ethyne)

preparation.note: Two successive HX eliminations. Use alcoholic KOH (not aqueous).

acidic_character.reason: sp hybridised C has 50% s character --> highest electronegativity --> H more easily released as H+
reactions: HC=CH + Na --> HC=C(-) Na(+) + 1/2 H2 (Monosodium ethynide)
acidic_character.trap: Internal alkynes (but-2-yne) have NO terminal H on sp C --> do NOT react with Na or NaNH2

addition_reactions.note: gem = same carbon. Follows Markovnikov rule.

addition_reactions.trap: Students write ethanol as product — WRONG. It gives ETHANAL via tautomerism.

linear.use: Battery electrodes (conducts electricity under special conditions)

cyclic.importance: Best route from aliphatic to aromatic compounds

benzene.combustion: Burns with SOOTY/LUMINOUS flame (high C:H ratio = 1:1)
benzene.reactivity: Prefers SUBSTITUTION over addition (to preserve aromaticity)

kekule_limitations: Predicted 2 ortho-disubstituted isomers — but only 1 found experimentally
Failed to explain extra stability of benzene

resonance.description: Benzene = resonance hybrid of two Kekule structures
resonance.representation: Circle inside hexagon = 6 DELOCALISED pi electrons

orbital_picture.sigma_framework: 6 C-C sigma bonds + 6 C-H sigma bonds — all in one plane
orbital_picture.pi_system: 6 unhybridised p orbitals perpendicular to ring plane
orbital_picture.pi_overlap: Forms 2 doughnut-shaped electron clouds (above and below the ring)
orbital_picture.why_substitution: Addition destroys aromaticity = loss of ~150 kJ/mol stability. Substitution preserves aromaticity.

conditions: Complete delocalisation of pi electrons in the ring

step1.name: Electrophile Generation
examples: HNO3 + H2SO4 --> NO2+ + HSO4- (nitronium ion for nitration)

step2.name: Arenium Ion (sigma-complex) Formation
step2.description: E+ attacks benzene ring --> one C becomes sp3 --> sigma-complex forms. Aromaticity LOST.

step3.name: Proton Removal
step3.description: AlCl4- removes H+ from sp3 carbon --> AlCl3 regenerated --> aromaticity RESTORED

BHC_formation.reaction: Benzene + 3Cl2 --[UV light, 500K]--> C6H6Cl6 (BHC / Benzene hexachloride / Gammaxane)
BHC_formation.important: ADDITION reaction (not substitution). UV light, NOT Lewis acid.
BHC_formation.trap: Students write BHC as substitution — WRONG. It is FREE RADICAL ADDITION under UV light.

directive_influence.description: Nature of substituent already on ring decides where next group enters

groups.reason: Lone pair donation (+M effect) increases e-density at ortho and para

groups.trap: MOST COMMON NEET TRAP — Halogens are o/p directors despite being deactivating

groups.reason: Withdraw electrons from ring; o,p positions become electron-poor; electrophile attacks meta

carcinogenicity.description: Polynuclear aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) with 2+ fused benzene rings are CARCINOGENIC
carcinogenicity.source: Incomplete combustion of tobacco, coal, petroleum
carcinogenicity.effect: Enter body --> biochemical reactions --> damage DNA --> cause cancer

markovnikov_vs_anti_markovnikov.anti_markovnikov: NO peroxide effect for HCl or HI

cis_vs_trans.cis: Same groups SAME side of C=C

ortho_para_vs_meta_directors.meta: Decreases at o and p more than meta

exceptions_and_special_cases.exception: Methane CANNOT be made by Kolbe electrolysis
exceptions_and_special_cases.explanation: Formate gives only CO2, no CH3 radical

exceptions_and_special_cases.exception: Iodination of alkanes needs HIO3 or HNO3
exceptions_and_special_cases.explanation: I2 reaction is reversible; oxidising agent shifts equilibrium
F-F bond too reactive; complete degradation occurs
H-Cl too strong, H-I too weak for radical chain

exceptions_and_special_cases.exception: Lindlar catalyst stops at alkene stage
Internal alkynes do NOT react with NaNH2
exceptions_and_special_cases.explanation: Only terminal sp C-H is acidic
Addition destroys aromaticity = loss of ~150 kJ/mol

exceptions_and_special_cases.exception: Halogens are o/p directors despite deactivating
exceptions_and_special_cases.explanation: +M effect (lone pair donation) overrides -I for directing
UV light + Cl2; free radical mechanism; no Lewis acid

exceptions_and_special_cases.exception: trans isomer has higher melting point than cis
exceptions_and_special_cases.explanation: More symmetrical structure --> better crystal packing

exceptions_and_special_cases.exception: Wurtz with 2 different R-X gives mixture
exceptions_and_special_cases.explanation: R-R, R-R' and R'-R' all form (not selective)

exceptions_and_special_cases.exception: C-C bond in benzene = 139 pm (not 133 or 154)
exceptions_and_special_cases.explanation: Resonance hybrid -- intermediate between C-C and C=C

memory_tricks.explanation: H adds to carbon that ALREADY HAS MORE H atoms (the rich carbon gets richer). Br adds to the poor carbon (fewer H, more substituted).
Anti-Markovnikov = Only HBr + Peroxide. NOT HCl (bond too strong), NOT HI (I radical recombines). Bromine is the only rebel halide in radical reactions.

memory_tricks.trick: L FOR LIKE = SAME SIDE = CIS
memory_tricks.explanation: Lindlar = Like = Same side = CIS. Na/liq.NH3 = TRANS (opposite sides).
-OH, -NH2 (amine), -NHR, -NR2, -OCH3, Alkyl (-CH3, -C2H5). All donate electrons by resonance (+M) --> ortho/para directors.

memory_tricks.trick: NO CN CHO COR COOH SO3H = METAL THIEVES
memory_tricks.explanation: -NO2, -CN, -CHO, -COR, -COOH, -COOR, -SO3H. All withdraw electrons from ring --> meta directors. They are electron thieves.
n=0: 2pi | n=1: 6pi (benzene) | n=2: 10pi (naphthalene) | n=3: 14pi. Quick check: Is the number 2, 6, 10, 14...? Then aromatic.
Staggered = S for Safe and Stable (most stable, least strain). Eclipsed = E for Exhausted (highest energy, least stable). Energy diff = 12.5 kJ/mol.

memory_tricks.trick: BREAK THE BOND, BOTH SIDES GET C=O
memory_tricks.explanation: Each C of C=C becomes carbonyl: Terminal =CH2 --> HCHO | =CHR --> RCHO (aldehyde) | =CR2 --> ketone
sp (alkyne) > sp2 (alkene) > sp3 (alkane) in acidity. More s-character = more electronegative C = weaker C-H = more acidic.

memory_tricks.trick: F Cl Br I = Fast Cat But Idle
memory_tricks.explanation: F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2. F = Ferocious (too fast, uncontrollable), I = Idle (too slow, reversible).

boiling_point_alkane_isomers.reason: More branching = more spherical = less surface area = lower bp

tier1_must_memorize.key_fact: ONLY HBr shows peroxide effect; gives 1-bromopropane from propene
Each C of C=C becomes carbonyl
Ethane byproduct from termination CH3. + CH3.
Terminal sp C-H most acidic; internal alkynes not acidic
Both C of C=C must have 2 DIFFERENT groups
Methane cannot be made; gives even-carbon alkanes

tier2_high_probability.key_point: Product has 1 less C than acid

tier3_trap_zone.trap_question: cis has higher m.p. or b.p.?
tier3_trap_zone.correct_answer: cis higher b.p.; trans higher m.p.

mcq_test.question: Addition of HBr to propene in presence of benzoyl peroxide gives:
mcq_test.explanation: Peroxide effect (anti-Markovnikov) with HBr only. Br radical adds to terminal C (less substituted). 2° radical more stable than 1° radical.

mcq_test.question: Which catalyst gives trans-alkene from an alkyne?
mcq_test.explanation: Lindlar catalyst gives CIS alkene (syn addition). Na/liquid NH3 gives TRANS alkene (anti addition). Most frequently tested NEET question.

mcq_test.question: Ethane is obtained as a byproduct during chlorination of methane due to:
options: D) Termination step: CH3. + CH3. --> C2H6
mcq_test.explanation: In termination step, two methyl radicals combine to give ethane. This explains ethane as byproduct in methane chlorination.

mcq_test.question: Which of the following is an ortho and para director?
mcq_test.explanation: Halogens (-Cl, -Br, -F, -I) are ortho/para directors despite being deactivating. This is the most common trap in NEET. -NO2, -CN, -COOH are meta directors.

mcq_test.question: Product of hydration of propyne with Hg2+/H2SO4 at 333K is:
mcq_test.explanation: CH3-C=CH + H2O --> CH3-C(OH)=CH2 (vinyl alcohol) --[tautomerism]--> CH3-CO-CH3 (propanone). Markovnikov: OH adds to middle C of propyne.

mcq_test.question: Aromaticity requires:
options: D) Alternating single and double bonds
mcq_test.explanation: Huckel rule: aromatic = (4n+2) pi electrons + planar + complete delocalisation. 4n = anti-aromatic. Non-benzenoid aromatics also exist.

mcq_test.question: Ozonolysis of 2-methylbut-2-ene gives:
mcq_test.explanation: 2-Methylbut-2-ene = (CH3)2C=CH-CH3. Cleavage at C=C: (CH3)2C= gives propanone. =CH-CH3 gives ethanal.

mcq_test.question: Decarboxylation of sodium propanoate gives:
mcq_test.explanation: CH3CH2COONa + NaOH --[CaO]--> CH3CH3 + Na2CO3. Product has 1 C less than acid. Propanoic acid (3C) --> ethane (2C). TRAP: students say propane.

mcq_test.question: Wurtz reaction is NOT suitable for preparing:
mcq_test.explanation: Two different alkyl halides give mixture of R-R, R-R' and R'-R'. Not selective for odd carbon or mixed halide reactions.

mcq_test.question: Most stable conformation of ethane is:
mcq_test.explanation: Staggered: H atoms farthest apart, minimum electron cloud repulsion, minimum torsional strain. 12.5 kJ/mol less energy than eclipsed.

top_20_reactions.reaction: Ozonolysis: C=C + O3 then Zn/H2O --> aldehydes/ketones
Alkene + cold dil. KMnO4 (Baeyer) --> vicinal diol
Kolbe: RCOO-Na+ (electrolysis) --> R-R at anode (EVEN carbon)
Benzene + fuming H2SO4 --> Benzene sulphonic acid (sulphonation)

top_20_one_liners: Alkanes = CnH2n+2 | sp3 | 109.5° | 154 pm C-C | paraffins (inert)
Alkenes = CnH2n | sp2 | ~120° | 134 pm C=C | olefins (oil forming)
Alkynes = CnH2n-2 | sp | 180° | 120 pm C=C | linear | most acidic C-H
Markovnikov: H+ to C with MORE H atoms (forms more stable carbocation)
Anti-Markovnikov (peroxide): ONLY HBr | free radical | 1-bromopropane from propene
Lindlar = CIS alkene | Na/liq.NH3 = TRANS alkene (from alkynes)
Ozonolysis: each C of C=C becomes C=O | terminal =CH2 gives HCHO
Huckel Rule: (4n+2) pi electrons + planar + delocalised = AROMATIC
Benzene: 139 pm C-C | sp2 | 6 pi electrons (delocalised) | prefers EAS
Halogens = DEACTIVATING but ORTHO/PARA directors — NEET TRAP
-NO2, -COOH, -CN = META directors + deactivating
-OH, -NH2, -OCH3 = ACTIVATING + ORTHO/PARA directors
BHC = Benzene + 3Cl2 (UV, 500K) — ADDITION not substitution
Staggered more stable than eclipsed | energy diff = 12.5 kJ/mol
cis: higher b.p., lower m.p. | trans: lower b.p., higher m.p.
Internal alkynes NOT acidic (no terminal H on sp C)
Methane CANNOT be prepared by Kolbe electrolysis
Ethane = BYPRODUCT of methane chlorination (termination: CH3. + CH3.)
Wurtz: useful only for EVEN carbon, SAME alkyl halide
Acidic order: Alkyne > Alkene > Alkane (sp > sp2 > sp3 C-H)

top_20_mistakes.correct: trans has higher m.p.; cis has higher b.p.
BHC = addition reaction under UV light

top_20_mistakes.mistake: Wurtz works for odd C alkanes
top_20_mistakes.correct: Mixed halides give mixture; not selective for odd C
sp C-H (alkyne) most acidic; sp3 least acidic
EAS: E+ attacks pi cloud first --> arenium ion --> H+ removed

top_20_ncert_hidden_facts: Alkanes called 'paraffins' = parum affinis (little affinity in Latin)
Ethene called 'ethylene' because it gives oily liquid with Cl2
CH4 found in coal mines and marshy places (marsh gas)
LPG = liquefied petroleum gas | CNG = compressed natural gas
Benzene isolated by Michael Faraday in 1825
Kekule proposed benzene structure in 1865 (after dream of snake biting its own tail)
X-ray diffraction confirms all 6 C-C bonds in benzene = 139 pm (equal)
Carbon black (from incomplete combustion) used in ink, printer ink, pigments, filters
Petrol/lower petroleum fractions used for dry cleaning (removes grease stains)
Like dissolves like: polar in polar; non-polar in non-polar solvents
Boiling point: straight chain alkane > branched (more branching = less surface area)
Kharash and Mayo discovered peroxide effect in 1933 at University of Chicago
Markovnikov proposed his rule in 1869
Torsional strain = repulsion between adjacent C-H bond electron clouds
Conformations have SAME bond angles and SAME bond lengths (only spatial arrangement changes)
Staggered and eclipsed represented by Sawhorse or Newman projection
Polyacetylene films (from ethyne) used as battery electrodes
Naphthalene balls: moth repellent used in toilets (characteristic aromatic smell)
Benzene burns with sooty/luminous flame (high C:H ratio = 6:6 = 1:1)
PAHs from incomplete combustion of tobacco, coal, petroleum damage DNA and cause cancer

top_20_expected_neet_questions.topic: trans vs cis: which has higher m.p.?
Which alkyne does not react with NaNH2?

top_20_expected_neet_questions.answer_hook: Addition of Cl2 to benzene under UV (free radical)
